steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The sentence sets up a contrast with "but": Velázquez was Spain's court painter, yet something about his influence and Spain is contradicted by the fact that painters "around the world" used his techniques. The blank must describe a geographic limitation that the "around the world" clause contradicts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'derived from': "His influence was hardly derived from Spain" would mean his influence did not come from Spain — but the passage implies he was indeed a Spanish court painter, so Spain is the source. This contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'recognized in': "His influence was hardly recognized in Spain" would mean Spain did not appreciate him, but the text says he was the leading artist in the Spanish court. This contradicts the opening. ✗
  • Choice C — 'confined to': "His influence was hardly confined to Spain" means his influence was not limited to Spain — exactly what the rest of the sentence confirms: painters around the world employed his techniques. The "but" contrast works perfectly here. ✓
  • Choice D — 'repressed by': "His influence was hardly repressed by Spain" makes no logical sense in context. There is no suggestion that Spain tried to suppress him. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
